back_translation: 'This is what you shall do to them to consecrate them to serve me as priests: take one young bull and two rams without blemish,'
'and unleavened bread, unleavened cakes mixed with oil, and unleavened wafers anointed with oil. Make them of fine wheat flour.'
'Put them in one basket and present them in the basket, along with the bull and the two rams.'
'Aaron and his sons you shall bring to the entrance of the Tent of Meeting, and wash them with water.'
'Take the garments and clothe Aaron with the tunic, the robe of the ephod, the ephod itself, and the breastpiece; fasten the ephod on him with the woven waistband of the ephod.'
'Put the turban on his head and place the holy diadem on the turban.'
'Take the anointing oil, pour it on his head, and anoint him.'
'Present his sons and clothe them with tunics.'
'Gird them with sashes — Aaron and his sons — and bind headbands on them. The priesthood shall be theirs by a perpetual statute. So you shall ordain Aaron and his sons.'
'You shall bring the bull before the Tent of Meeting, and Aaron and his sons shall lay their hands on the head of the bull.'
'You shall slaughter the bull before the LORD at the entrance of the Tent of Meeting.'
'Take some of the blood of the bull and put it on the horns of the altar with your finger, and pour all the blood at the base of the altar.'
'Take all the fat that covers the entrails, the lobe over the liver, the two kidneys with the fat that is on them, and burn them on the altar.'
'But the flesh of the bull and its hide and its dung you shall burn with fire outside the camp; it is a sin offering.'
'You shall take one of the rams, and Aaron and his sons shall lay their hands on the head of the ram.'
'You shall slaughter the ram, take its blood, and sprinkle it on all sides of the altar.'
'Cut the ram into its pieces, wash its entrails and its legs, and put them with its pieces and its head.'
'You shall burn the whole ram on the altar; it is a burnt offering to the LORD — a pleasing aroma, a food offering to the LORD.'
'You shall take the second ram, and Aaron and his sons shall lay their hands on the head of the ram.'

'You shall slaughter the ram and take some of its blood and put it on the right earlobe of Aaron and on the right earlobes of his sons, on the thumbs of their right hands, and on the big toes of their right feet; and you shall sprinkle the blood on all sides of the altar.'

'Take some of the blood that is on the altar and some of the anointing oil and sprinkle it on Aaron and on his garments, and on his sons and on the garments of his sons with him. He and his garments shall be consecrated, and his sons and the garments of his sons with him.'
'Take from the ram the fat, the fat tail, the fat covering the entrails, the lobe of the liver, both kidneys with the fat on them, and the right thigh — for it is a ram of ordination.'
'And one loaf of bread, one cake of bread of oil, and one wafer from the basket of unleavened bread that is before the LORD.'
'Put all of it on the palms of Aaron and on the palms of his sons, and wave them as a wave offering before the LORD.'
'Then take them from their hands and burn them on the altar atop the burnt offering, for a pleasing aroma before the LORD — it is a food offering to the LORD.'
'Take the breast from the ram of ordination that is for Aaron and wave it as a wave offering before the LORD, and it shall be your portion.'

'You shall consecrate the breast of the wave offering and the thigh of the contribution, which is waved and which is lifted up, from the ram of ordination — from what is Aaron's and what is his sons'.'

'This shall belong to Aaron and his sons as a perpetual statute from the sons of Israel, for it is a contribution; it shall be a contribution from the sons of Israel from their peace-offering sacrifices — their contribution to the LORD.'
'The holy garments of Aaron shall belong to his sons after him, for anointing in them and for ordaining in them.'
'Seven days the priest in his place from his sons shall wear them, the one who comes into the Tent of Meeting to minister in the Holy Place.'
'You shall take the ram of ordination and boil its flesh in a holy place.'
'Aaron and his sons shall eat the flesh of the ram and the bread that is in the basket at the entrance of the Tent of Meeting.'
'They shall eat those things by which atonement was made — to ordain them, to consecrate them. But an outsider shall not eat them, for they are holy.'
'If any of the flesh of ordination or any of the bread remains until morning, you shall burn the remainder with fire; it shall not be eaten, for it is holy.'
'So you shall do for Aaron and his sons, according to all that I have commanded you. Seven days you shall ordain them.'
'A bull as a sin offering you shall offer each day for atonement; you shall purify the altar by making atonement upon it, and you shall anoint it to consecrate it.'
'Seven days you shall make atonement upon the altar and consecrate it, and the altar shall be most holy; whatever touches the altar shall be holy.'
'This is what you shall offer on the altar: two year-old lambs every day continually.'
'The one lamb you shall offer in the morning, and the second lamb you shall offer at twilight.'
'With the first lamb, a tenth of fine flour mixed with a quarter of a hin of beaten oil, and a quarter of a hin of wine as a drink offering.'
'The second lamb you shall offer at twilight, with the same grain offering and the same drink offering as in the morning — a pleasing aroma, a food offering to the LORD.'
'A continual burnt offering throughout your generations, at the entrance of the Tent of Meeting before the LORD, where I will meet with you to speak to you there.'
'I will meet there with the sons of Israel, and it shall be consecrated by my glory.'
'I will consecrate the Tent of Meeting and the altar, and Aaron and his sons I will consecrate to serve me as priests.'
'I will dwell among the sons of Israel and will be their God.'
'They shall know that I am the LORD their God, who brought them out of the land of Egypt so that I might dwell among them. I am the LORD their God.'
